fullArticle:
EXCLUSIVE: Greenwich Entertainment has acquired U.S. distribution rights to A Girl Unknown, the debut feature by Chinese filmmaker Zou Jing.

The movie premiered in the Critics Weeks section of the 2026 Cannes Film Festival where it won the Fipresci Prize and the Gan Foundation Award. This week, the Munich International Film Festival awarded it the CineVision competition prize for Best International Film by an Emerging Director.

Following a “tour” of North American fall film festivals, Greenwich plans a theatrical release in early 2027.

The synopsis reads: “Unfolding over 15 years during the peak of China’s strict One Child policy with its traumatic impact on young girls, A Girl Unknown is a poignant, naturalistic coming-of-age story that traces the displacement and resilience of an adop...
